name: Riopudio Green Corridor Trail
name_original: Arroyo y ribera del Río Pudio — circular desde Mairena del Aljarafe
description: The Stream and riverbank of the Río Pudio — circular from Mairena del Aljarafe is an easy 4.5-mile (7.3 km) hike along the Río Pudio, featur
region_intro: Hiking around Bormujos offers accessible outdoor spaces within the Aljarafe region of Seville. The area is characterized by gently rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and agricultural land. The Corredor Verde del Río Pudio provides diverse riparian landscapes along the Pudio River. Bormujos also features ample green spaces, including Los Álamos Park and Parque de la Alquería, for leisurely exploration. Best hiking trails around Bormujos The most popular hiking route is Riopudio Green Corridor Trail, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy path follows the natural surroundings of the Pudio River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from Bormujos, an easy 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route features gentle elevation changes through the local landscape. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Tomares, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through the Aljarafe countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Bormujos is defined by rolling hills, olive groves, and the riparian landscapes of the Pudio River. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate paths. The routes in Bormujos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bormujos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bormujos?
A: Bormujos offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 300 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more moderate paths through the Aljarafe region.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on hikes around Bormujos?
A: Hikes around Bormujos are characterized by the gently rolling hills and extensive olive groves typical of the Aljarafe region. You'll also find diverse riparian landscapes along the Corredor Verde del Río Pudio, offering a refreshing natural escape. The area provides a blend of agricultural scenery and green spaces.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Bormujos?
A: Yes, Bormujos is ideal for beginners and those seeking relaxed walks. Over 180 easy trails are available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1007751872/riopudio-green-corridor-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riopudio Green Corridor Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.5-mile (7.3 km) path that follows the natural surroundings of the Pudio River.

Q: Are there longer or more challenging hikes available in Bormujos?
A: While Bormujos is known for its accessible trails, there are options for those looking for more moderate distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3953620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Castilleja loop from Ciudad Expo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.2-mile (14.8 km) route that provides a longer exploration of the local landscape.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Bormujos?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Bormujos are designed as loops, making them convenient for hikers. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17225936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Bormujos&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.6-mile (7.4 km) path featuring gentle elevation changes through the local scenery.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Bormujos suitable for families?
A: Absolutely. The accessible nature of Bormujos's trails, particularly those along the Corredor Verde del Río Pudio and within local parks like Los Álamos Park, make them very suitable for families. Many routes are easy and offer pleasant, safe environments for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Bormujos?
A: Generally, the green spaces and natural corridors around Bormujos are welcoming to dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near agricultural land, and to clean up after your pet.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bormujos?
A: The trails in Bormujos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene natural settings, the ease of access, and the well-maintained paths, particularly along the Pudio River.

Q: Are there any notable parks or green spaces to explore in Bormujos?
A: Bormujos boasts several significant green areas. &lt;strong&gt;Los Álamos Park&lt;/strong&gt; is a substantial 15-hectare space perfect for leisurely strolls. The &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Botánico&lt;/strong&gt; offers a unique interactive garden experience, and &lt;strong&gt;Parque de la Alquería&lt;/strong&gt; provides another large green lung to the south of the town.

Q: What attractions are near Bormujos that I could visit after a hike?
A: While Bormujos itself offers natural beauty, its proximity to Seville means you can easily visit iconic landmarks. These include the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seville Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza of Spain&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Gold&lt;/a&gt;. For natural attractions, consider exploring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María Luisa Park&lt;/a&gt; in Seville.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking areas in Bormujos?
A: Bormujos is well-connected within the Aljarafe region and to Seville by public transport, primarily bus services. These can help you reach various points within the town, from which many trails, especially those in the Corredor Verde del Río Pudio or local parks, are easily accessible on foot.

Q: Are there viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails in Bormujos?
A: The trails through the Aljarafe region and along the Corredor Verde del Río Pudio offer picturesque views of olive groves, agricultural landscapes, and the riparian environment. While there aren't dramatic mountain viewpoints, you'll find serene spots to enjoy the natural beauty and tranquility of the area.
tips: The El Azud mill dam is located right on the Repudio stream, where next to it there is an area to eat and be. A recommended visit to learn about the 'unknown' heritage of Mairena del Aljarafe.
Path that follows the torrent that descends until reaching the Río Guadalquivir. Naturalized space where you can walk on both sides of the same river.
Remodeled bridge in 2002 that crosses the Pudio river. There is a wooded area with picnic areas and, very close, a lot of fig trees that give away delicious brevas and figs every year.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hiking trail, suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. The paths are generally easy to navigate.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The hike typically takes up to 2 hours to complete, offering a leisurely outdoor experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of gravel paths (about 6.63 km) and natural paths (around 526 meters), with very small sections of unpaved or paved tracks. The paths are generally easy to navigate.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter the Mill weir (Azud del Molino) early on, a historic mill dam with a resting area. Further along, you'll follow the Arroyo del Repudio Trail, and near the end, you'll find the Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River, which has a wooded section with picnic spots and numerous fig trees.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as you may enjoy a diverse display of wildflowers and fruit trees like almond, plum, and fig trees. While the river's water flow can be minimal in summer, it increases significantly after heavy rains.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail's starting point is conveniently located next to a parking lot in Mairena del Aljarafe, making access straightforward.

faqs.question: Is the Stream and riverbank of the Río Pudio trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly, offering a pleasant environment for walks with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control, and check local regulations for leash requirements.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Corredor Verde del Riopudio?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike this trail or to access the Corredor Verde del Riopudio. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can I expect to see along the Río Pudio riverbank?
faqs.answer: Along the riverbanks, you might spot riparian vegetation such as white poplar, reed, and bulrush. The surrounding landscape features agricultural areas, especially olive groves, and in spring, you can see diverse wildflowers and fruit trees like almond, plum, and fig trees.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Corredor Verde del Riopudio and the Cañada Real de las Islas.

faqs.question: Are there any specific considerations for cyclists on this path?
faqs.answer: Hikers should be aware that cyclists also frequently use these paths, sometimes mistaking them for competition tracks. Please share the trail respectfully.

waypoints.name: Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River
